column1: # Pinned upstream CIP example fixtures used by CI to detect canonicalization
# drift in the cardano-signer + perl-control-char-escape pipeline that
# scripts/metadata-canonize.sh wraps.
# Each row: <id> <safemode> <sha-pinned-url> <golden-canonized-hash>
# - id            short identifier; CI uses it as a download filename
# - safemode      yes|no — passed through to cardano-signer canonize. CIP-119 and
#                 some CIP-136 documents use body shapes that cardano-signer
#                 1.27.0 does not recognise as CIP-108-compatible and rejects
#                 under safe mode; --disable-safemode lets canonization proceed
#                 deterministically.
# - URL           PINNED to a commit SHA, not a branch. Bumping the SHA is a
#                 deliberate act and requires re-computing the golden hash.
# - golden hash   blake2b-256 of the canonized body, hex. If CI fails this,
#                 the cause is one of: cardano-signer version drift, perl-escape
#                 behavior drift, the pinned upstream file changed under the
#                 same SHA (should not be possible), or a wrapper-script change.
#                 Investigate the cause; do NOT just bump the golden.
# Why no schema-validation step here? The upstream cardano-foundation/CIPs
# repository's published example .jsonld files do not currently conform to the
# repository's own published schemas (e.g. CIP-100 schema enum on
# witnessAlgorithm is ["ed25519"] only, but CIP-108 examples use "CIP-0008";
# CIP-108 schema requires `name` on each author, but examples omit it). That's
# an upstream inconsistency outside this repo's scope to fix. CI runs
# metadata-validate.sh against the in-repo `test/*.jsonld` fixtures separately.
# Pinned to cardano-foundation/CIPs commit
#   5c29bb73d8ae90bfcea9c3d20849c90700b99ff4
# Re-pin by editing this file; the rest of CI reads from here.
# Lines starting with # and blank lines are ignored. Tab-separated fields.
cip108-treasury-withdrawal
cip108-no-confidence
cip119-drep
cip136-treasury-withdrawal-unconstitutional
cip136-parameter-change-abstain
